title: JRN Probe Cost-Efficiency Replication on rel-stack (11 FK Joins, 3 Tasks)
summary: Demo notebook replicating Join Reproduction Number (JRN) probe cost-efficiency analysis on the RelBench rel-stack dataset (Stack Exchange, 7 tables, 11 FK joins, 4.2M rows). Evaluates convergence of cheap probes vs full-budget models, compares JRN-based join selection against greedy forward selection and random baselines across 3 tasks (user-engagement, user-badge, post-votes). Key finding: JRN selection achieves 98.8–100% of greedy performance but with only 5–6 direct joins the speedup remains below 1×, validating the theoretical breakeven at J≈8. Mini data JSON contains per-task convergence stats, JRN values, greedy/JRN/random performance curves, and cost-efficiency metrics.
